cover.title: UQ Course Cover Maker
cover.subtitle: Course review / course combo → live preview → export a portrait image (1080×1440)
cover.contentPlaceholder: Paste your long review; line breaks split paragraphs
cover.contentHint: Split by paragraph automatically; overflow flows to the next image, with a page number bottom-right
cover.contentPages: Auto split into {{n}} image(s); export saves them one by one
cover.quoteHint: 2–3 lines recommended, keep each line short
cover.noteTitlePlaceholder: Title shown under the cover in the feed
cover.noteTitleHint: Only used in the feed preview on the right, not on the cover image
cover.paletteLabel: Decoration palette (accent bar / lines / title highlight)
cover.schemeHint: Follows the diffusion background by default; pick a preset to override
cover.textColorLabel: Text color (independent from palette; pick white for white text)
cover.photoHint: Use your own UQ photo (portrait works best). Processed on your device only, never uploaded to any server.
cover.previewNote: The preview is scaled down; the export is a 2160×2880 HD JPG (~0.5MB). Color and text changes update live.

xhs.banner: Preview how the cover looks on the home / search feed

default.noteTitle: UQ INFS7410 | A course that forces you to truly get it
default.name: Information Retrieval
default.quote:
Medium difficulty, but it forces you off AI
and makes you truly understand IR and RAG
Great pick to get into IR ✅
default.content:
The pace is fast overall. By week three it had already covered retrieval and the vector space model, with assignments piling on right after, so do not fall behind from the start.

The biggest gain is that it forces you off ready-made AI tools and makes you implement TF-IDF and BM25 yourself, so you truly understand the ranking behind the scenes instead of just calling an API.

Assessment has three parts: two assignments, one project, and a final. The project weighs the most, so form a team early; reliable teammates matter more than anything.

In one line: if you want to get into information retrieval and RAG for real, this course is worth it, but do not expect an easy high grade.
